prompt: The record does not validate its input correctly. What is the bug?
choices: Compact constructors cannot modify parameter values
Silently clamping invalid input to 0 hides the error; callers passing 150 receive a Percent(0) without any indication that their input was invalid; throw IllegalArgumentException instead to fail fast
The record will not compile because value is reassigned in the compact constructor
Records automatically clamp numeric fields to valid ranges
explanation: Silently replacing bad input with a default value hides bugs — the caller has no way to know their value was discarded. Records should enforce their invariants by throwing on invalid input. Fix: throw new IllegalArgumentException("value must be 0-100, got " + value); so callers are alerted immediately.
